reviews.review_msg: One of the best professors teaching STATS coming from a crim major. Her lectures are kind of run on and you might think skipping lecture is okay but I actually learnt a lot from her, I think She explains concepts very well compared to other profs. The other reviews seem bad butassignments gave direct instructions and were not difficult to complete.

Girl why am I spending 3 hours on the weekly homework just for it to be worth 1% each ??? Marie is a very nice person but her lectures are unorganized. Make sure to put R question examples on your cheat sheet because she puts it on the exam. If R was a person I would brutally murder it. Still don't know what a confidence interval is.

This is a stats course for those who are in the social sciences, and likely don't have a ton of math experience. It was an absolute nightmare. The homework is insane and takes hours to complete. Her exams are horrible and defeating. I tried my level best and still barely passed. Avoid at all costs

One of the messiest profs i have had so far, not easy to follow and expects a lot from her students when half the time her slides are incorrect and full of errors. Midterm av was 52%, instead of going over the concepts she said how disappointed she was in us. Avoid her at all costs, weekly 3hr assignments with coding you must teach urself worth 10%

she's nice but can't teach and her lectures are confusing. she says something and then corrects herself a few slides later or she'll have an example on the slide but then it says "check my work" or something... like miss girl YOU check it! there's too many platforms that are used like clicker, achieve, canvas, stat workshop and crowd mark.

She cares about students, and is a very nice person but she CANNOT TEACH. She would often make mistakes in lecture and send 10 emails throughout the week correcting herself. Assignments were worth very little towards the grade, but they were a lot of work. Overall, a nice person but I would not take this class with her!

Do not take this course with her!! It's an overall heavy class with many concepts and things to learn. She's actually very sweet and seems to care about her students but she does not know how to teach and convey information. She is a tough grader and gives out so many assignments that are excessively long. She cannot teach!

Her class is very hard because it is statistics that is meant to be for people in social sciences. Less than half the class would be at lectures and therefore most people failed the midterm. If you take her class you need to attend the lectures every week to understand what is going on. The start of the class is pretty easy but it gets much harder

Please avoid this prof at all costs!!! Extremely unclear and has no idea what she is doing 95% of the time. Gives so much homework for it to be worth only 10% in total. Her midterm average was absolutely horrible and then wonders why as if she is not the issue. She is generally nice but a terrible prof. Please avoid for your own sanity.

Marie was very nice and it was obvious that she had good intentions. I didn't find her lectures helpful and instead relied mostly on the textbook (which was very useful). Assignments were very similar to how she formatted her exams, but were time consuming and only worth 12%. Wouldn't recommend unless you want to teach yourself the material.

Marie is super sweet however her teaching was crazy. She will speed run through lectures and teach the wrong materials and then will try and correct herself later. You will have homework to do all the time but each assignment is only worth 2% of your grade. She is a nice person but would avoid taking a class with her!

This professor is a nice person but was very disorganized this term. She would constantly make mistakes in lecture and then send 7 emails correcting herself. The assignments are too long, redundant, and the marking is inconsistent. The tests are worth too much and the only good thing she did was yelling off on people who were talking. Slay

she is a very kind person but a very disorganized prof. it is very difficult to learn in her class because she will teach you a concept incorrectly and then double back and reteach it. the assignments are worth about 2% of your grade but are very long. not impossible to succeed in this class but very difficult.

I am not one to leave negative reviews ever but, this prof really deserves it.This class had the potential to be enjoyable and interesting but, she makes it dreadful. The lack of explanation in her lectures and slides and her monotone voice makes it very difficult to attend class. Anyways, AVOID THIS PROF IF YOU HAVE ANY RESPECT FOR YOURSELF!!!!!!

Though we aren't marked for attendance, it would be tough if you missed class. Her slides occasionally have blank examples that she fills in during lecture that helped me later with practicing concepts. Assignments were ridiculously long for being only worth 12% total of our entire grade, with a 5% per HOUR late mark deduction.

The absolute worst professor at SFU by far. has no idea what shes teaching and often sends 15 emails a week correcting things she's said during lecture or on assignments. DO NOT TAKE ANY CLASS WITH HER

She often accidentally teaches the wrong answer for the wrong question and then corrects her self after realizing she taught us something else. Coding is barely covered in class and is heavily based on our homework assignments. She also accidentally gives us the wrong coding then, corrects herself later. If this wasn't a requirement, don't take.

Talked to her on the first day of class, and seemed nice. I found it very hard to keep this respect for her as the term went on. 


EXTREMELY DISORGANIZED!!! 

crazy short deadlines and her assignments are super messy and redundant

also refuses to post anything on canvas, so you need to basically create your own calender for this class

This prof is the worst professor at SFU. She is very disorganized and not helpful. Students sent her emails asking for help and she would say she was busy. She was not approachable. You basically have to teach yourself this course because she cant teach anything. If you have never taken stats dont take it with her because she is never helpful.

AVOID! The worst prof I've ever had. Very disorganized, no clear deadlines/grading criteria. Asks Qs that she never taught. No accommodating at all and things that she's the only one struggling with switch to online learning. THE WORST! P.S. She posts assgs/lectures randomly and gives very little time to complete those.

Prof Loughin is a very organized and detail-oriented Professor. She explains concepts very clearly and is always open to hearing her students' feedback and questions in and outside of her lecture time. She introduces R programming well and gives us a lot of practice homework to ensure that we have a solid knowledge of the programming language.

Theres a lot of assignments, but she is actually quite lenient on deadline. She answers emails promptly and her and TAs give lots of good feedback. She follows the book quite closely so its not a big deal if you miss a lecture. I would go to lecture if you arent great at reading R input though.

she assigned 4 assignments all due in one week and the following week we had a midterm. She thinks that you are only taking her class and will not compromise on deadlines. AVOID TAKING THIS CLASS WITH HER!! 2 hour lectures feel like 6 hours with her.

I found her lectures quite easy to understand - you just have to read the assigned text before class. Only issue was that she gave a crap ton of homework that required knowledge of R (which is not included on exams), but the exams were fair and we were allowed to bring in notes.
Marie is awesome! Assignments can be more work than they are worth, but tests are fair and she is thorough in her lectures.
she's decent

I heard bad things about her prior to this class. She was actually pretty good. Explained the concepts pretty clearly, the assignments helped with the midterm. She gives a free 1% Iclicker, drops your lowest assignment score and provided online quizzes that weren't for marks. All in all not to difficult and a pretty decent professor.

She's a caring person and helps you outside of class. BUT shes a horrible professor. Her lectures are all over the place with technical difficulties every 10 minutes, mistakes in her notes and in assignment questions . Posts assignments last minute with 5-7 questions that she's barely touched over in class every week. Midterms were alright tho

Marie Loughin is a great professor! I don't know where these negative reviews are coming from. I work hard as a student & actually show up to class (more than 50% of the class never showed up for lectures). If you study her lecture notes and understand concepts, you can easily get an A. Please do not listen to these comments below lol.

lectures can be boring. Lectures follow text but provide new examples. midterms and final allow CHEAT SHEETS! know interpretation for parameters, confidence/prediction intervals, F-tests and T-test. Overall fair midterms and final and marking is fair. Workshop for really helpful. 5-6 homework assignments and a lot of time given for them. Manageable

Nice prof but she didn't teach very well. She didn't explain things clearly and she doesn't post notes beforehand so its hard to take notes when she's rushing through slides half explaining things. I hated using R as well. But the way she teaches is more concept based so if you're bad with numbers you should be fine.

her class is alright, bonus mark for all midterms and final, and makes her exams fine. Class average was almost 70% for all the midterms. The only problem is the short time frame to do the midterm and assignment on jupyter was annoying to do using R The prof is always available after lectures to answer any questions, and tries pretty hard explain

Extremely caring as a person, but not a great professor. Made exams relatively easy but tbh I would rather write harder exams with a prof who can convey the information better. Homework takes way too much time and her notes can be messy when she adds too much stuff with her ipad stylus

Honestly, Marie made stats 201 A LOT easier than I had expected. Yeah, she's kind of all over the place and sucks with technology BUT, her exams are VERY STRAIGHT FORWARD and you can honestly score an A+ in her class if you care enough to study her slides. She is a kind person too!! Give her a chance!

Made topics more complicated than they needed to be with terrible lecture slides. Homework was tedious and took way too long for something that doesnt adequately prepare you for exams. (She even says in the syllabus assignments take 6-10 hours) More than half the time spent on homework was due to coding which she doesnt test you on.

This course was ridiculous, she was so scattered brain and her slides made no sense sometimes and were littered with mistakes. Moreover, the pace in which she went through her slides made me think that she was trying to break the world record for most slides gone through in a minute. The assignments were absolutely ridiculous too, took way too long

This was a frustrating course to take. She would whip through her lecture slides, skipping over important material very quickly. Her assignments required using code, which doesn't help us to practice for the tests at all... The assignments only really helped for interpretation, but no practice for using the formulas themselves. PICKY marker

she made it so much harder to do well than this class than should be. There was 172 of us in the class, she only gave 3 A+ because you needed a 95% as your final grade for an A+. The homework takes forever and is marked very specifically, so you will likely lose 5% off homework alone. Lost stupid marks on the final, she shouldnt teach

Gives several hours worth of homework every week as if that's the only class students are taking. Lectures are filled with mistakes and have to explain to her why those mistakes are wrong. Lectures are incredibly unorganized and hard to follow. Tests are too long, filled with irrelevant questions, and not marked fairly.

The worst prof ive had in university so far. Everything she says in lecture is wrong. She literally has atleast 20 mistakes in all her lecture slides. Asked her a question in lecture in class and she goes "oh ask me that after class". clearly shows low level of competency. Easy A but the prof makes it hard.
Just one comment. Gazillion number of mistakes on slides and everywhere else! Very confusing and frustrating! Wouldnt recommend her..

Decent prof, but her overall demeanour was awful. She was condescending, rude, and literally kicked me out of her class in front of hundreds of students for coming in late. Lectures bland, just make sure you to the homework and study for the midterms, even though they are open book.

She has no respect in the classroom because she's very soft-spoken.  Lecture notes are great and follow the textbook very closely.  Super easy subject matter.  She explained everything very clearly but people were too busy talking/showing up 45 minutes late to pay attention.  Sit in the front.

I like her, but absolutely hated the student body that was present. She's rather soft-spoken, so it was difficult to hear when students talked - which then encouraged her to stop lecture, which led to more disruptions, sadly. She means well, but students really lack respect. Homework is lengthy, but relates to exams. Pretty straightforward overall.
she's really nice, just not very good at teaching. read the book and you'll be fine bc its all open note
Honestly, many homeowk required and quizes are on everyweek

Poor teaching style, gets off topic and often gets confused with her own thoughts. She's a good person, she just isn't good at teaching this class. Luckily the material is easy to understand without her help.
She is very stubborn, when you're late for class she stops the entire lecture just to call out on that particular student and asks them to leave the class. Like dude this aint high school.

She stops her lectures every time a student is late or using their phone and says "you are disrupting the class". Does she not understand that taking time out of her lecture to call every student out is more disruptive and wastes more time? She has a very bad attitude and no respect for her students. I advise that you stay away from her.
She's pretty bad

Lecture notes  are unorganized i.e. do not have page numbers. Pre-quizzes are only opened for a short period of time. She is the only prof available in this semester.  Fortunately, the material from the textbook is relatively easy.
the best teacher i ever met

She is too worried about the use of phones, and people murmuring in her class. In a class of 500 students, it is extremely unlikely for it to be completely silent. She should get over herself. Not a good lecturer. Students will be lucky that the material is easy to learn through the textbook.
While she was't always certain of what she wanted to say and got confused during lectures the material itself was easy. I skipped 2 months of lectures and pulled off a B+ with minimal effort.

Marie Loughin is not a natural educator. She does not explain material very well, and there are several mistakes on her slides that add confusion. Read the textbook and do practice problems.  Assignments are long, but can prepare you for exams if you understand the concepts behind the problems.  Exams are very easy if you understand the concepts.

She reads right off her notes, which is material that the textbook covers far clearly. I emailed her a couple of times, and she brushes off your emails by referring you to the TAs in the workshop. There is about 5 homework assignments, and none of those questions prepare you for the midterms. Her answer key is super detailed. NOT RECOMMENDED!

Her lectures begin with a simple example from the textbook, which could easily be done on your own. Then she goes on to say that "I don't go over everything, so go read the chapter," yeah wtf right?But you HAVE TO show up, since she adds EXTRA material for the test. Average homework assignments are about 3000-4000 words. TAKE IT ONLINE!

Reads directly off slides. Confused by her own notes. Has way too many technical difficulties. Constantly annoyed. Assigns way too much homework in way too short of time. Midterms do not cover anything done in the homework. Way too disorganized. Would not take a class with her again. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. You're welcome.

Her motto is "learn by doing", so she assigns so much homework. Homework doesn't prepare you for exams. She makes errors in her lecture notes and confuses herself and the class. Seems very annoyed when students approach her for questions. It's almost like distance education because she teaches so poorly, but with extra material she throws in

You won't understand anything in lecture, but heres the kick! you need to come because she adds in more material than the text! Only class I've read everything assigned. Home work is RIDICULOUS 3.5k words on my 4th/5 with an average at minimum of 2.5k FOR STATS! Very unorganized. AVOID do yourself a favor, caused me to do worse in other classses

Reads off slides word for word, assigns 20-30 questions to be done in a week and only 5 questions are marked. Midterm was too long to complete in amount of given time. Wont reply to emails and doesn't seem to understand what she is talking about half the time.
Reads her slides verbatim, and sometimes confused by her own text. Seemed annoyed when students approached her for help. Will not reply to emails.
